label: 1701x–2 Legal assistance for foreclosure-related issues

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (hereafter in this section referred to as the “Secretary”) shall establish a program for making grants for providing a full range of foreclosure legal assistance to low- and moderate-income homeowners and tenants related to home ownership preservation, home foreclosure prevention, and tenancy associated with home foreclosure.

children.heading: Competitive allocation
children.label: b Competitive allocation
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate amounts made available for grants under this section to State and local legal organizations on the basis of a competitive process. For purposes of this subsection “State and local legal organizations” are those State and local organizations whose primary business or mission is to provide legal assistance.

children.heading: Priority to certain areas
children.label: c Priority to certain areas
children.text: In allocating amounts in accordance with subsection (b), the Secretary shall give priority consideration to State and local legal organizations that are operating in the 125 metropolitan statistical areas (as that term is defined by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget) with the highest home foreclosure rates.

children.label: A
children.text: homeowners of owner-occupied homes with mortgages in default, in danger of default, or subject to or at risk of foreclosure; and

children.label: B
children.text: tenants at risk of or subject to eviction as a result of foreclosure of the property in which such tenant resides.

children.heading: Commence use within 90 days
children.label: 2 Commence use within 90 days
children.text: Any State or local legal organization that receives financial assistance pursuant to this section shall begin using any financial assistance received under this section within 90 days after receipt of the assistance.

children.heading: Prohibition on class actions
children.label: 3 Prohibition on class actions
children.text: No funds provided to a State or local legal organization under this section may be used to support any class action litigation.

children.heading: Limitation on legal assistance
children.label: 4 Limitation on legal assistance
children.text: Legal assistance funded with amounts provided under this section shall be limited to mortgage-related default, eviction, or foreclosure proceedings, without regard to whether such foreclosure is judicial or nonjudicial.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 5 Effective date
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, this subsection shall take effect on .July 21, 2010

children.heading: Limitation on distribution of assistance
children.label: e Limitation on distribution of assistance
A
children.text: any organization which has been convicted for a violation under Federal law relating to an election for Federal office; or

children.label: B
children.text: any organization which employs applicable individuals.

children.label: i
children.text: employed by the organization in a permanent or temporary capacity;

children.label: ii
children.text: contracted or retained by the organization; or

children.label: iii
children.text: acting on behalf of, or with the express or apparent authority of, the organization; and

children.label: B
children.text: has been convicted for a violation under Federal law relating to an election for Federal office.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary $35,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2011 through 2012 for grants under this section.
